name: medusa-tools
description: NemoClaw/OpenClaw tool definitions for interacting with the Medusa 256³ Cellular Automata via REST API. Install these tools in your agent's toolset to give it direct access to Medusa's vital signs.

tools.name: check_medusa_health
tools.description: Check if the Medusa engine is alive and running. Returns engine status, latest snapshot age, and overall health.

tools.name: get_medusa_status
tools.description: Get current engine status including latest snapshot, age, size, and whether the engine is producing new data.

tools.name: get_medusa_census
tools.description: Get the current cell state census: counts of VOID, STRUCTURAL, COMPUTE, ENERGY, SENSOR cells. Also returns entropy, fitness, generation number, and lattice size.

tools.name: get_medusa_equanimity
tools.description: Get the Equanimity/Sage census: counts of Elders, Sages, Ancients, Legends. Includes max age, median age, mean age, percentiles, and the coordinates and stats of the top 5 eldest Sages.

tools.name: get_acoustic_heatmap
tools.description: Get the Acoustic Map: a 16x16x16 friction heatmap showing which sectors of the 256³ lattice are stressed (high friction/suffering) vs silent (Sage-dominated/equanimous).

tools.name: get_medusa_telemetry
tools.description: Get the latest raw telemetry data from the engine's 5-minute status report. Includes transition matrices, age histograms, and detailed state change statistics.

tools.name: download_snapshot
tools.description: Download the latest .npz snapshot file containing the complete 256³ lattice state and 8-channel memory grid. WARNING: Large file (~50MB).

tools.name: export_stl_geometry
tools.description: Export the current organism geometry as an STL mesh file suitable for 3D printing. Returns a downloadable .stl file (~28MB with 50K cell sample).

usage_examples.nemoclaw: nemoclaw --tools medusa-tools 'Check Medusa census and tell me how many Sages we have'
usage_examples.openclaw: Add medusa-tools.json to your OpenClaw tool registry, then ask: 'What is Medusa's current max age?'

notes.network: API accessible from any machine on 192.168.86.0/24 subnet
notes.security: Local network only — no authentication required for read endpoints
notes.rate_limit: No rate limiting — snapshots are loaded from disk per request
